lc_check.lc_validation_reasoning: The certificate contains 'Credit No 0026IMU23000002' in the Remarks field (box 5). The term 'Credit No' in conjunction with a documentary credit number constitutes a reference to a Letter of Credit. However, applying the CRITICAL RULE in avsnitt 2.2 (banktermskontext): The label 'Credit No' is a shortened form that could refer to a documentary credit. Checking against the explicit banking terms list: 'LC', 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit', 'LC No.', 'LC Number'. The term used is 'Credit No' — not one of the enumerated explicit banking term labels. While 'Credit No' likely refers to a documentary credit in context, the strict rule requires one of the EXPLICIT banking term labels listed in 2.2. 'Credit No' is not identical to any of the listed terms ('LC', 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit', 'LC No.', 'LC Number'). Therefore, per the strict rule, the LC exception is NOT activated. Full verification per section 4 shall be performed.

criteria_checked.details: Certificate consignor 'Esswell International AB' is explicitly found in invoice header branding, body signature line, and footer as 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB'.
Certificate consignee 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED' matches invoice Customer/Applicant 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED' exactly.
Certificate shipping marks contain 'Esswell 21386' which matches invoice order number '21386'. Certificate does not contain an explicit invoice number field, but the order reference matches.

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignor 'Esswell International AB' found explicitly in invoice at multiple locations: (1) header branding 'ESSWELL International', (2) body signature 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB', (3) footer 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB'. After normalization of case, the company name is identical.

comparison_steps.description: Verify consignor country Sweden against invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice footer contains explicit address 'Gothenburg, Sweden' and VAT number 'SE556522640301' with SE prefix confirming Sweden. Certificate states 'Sweden'. Country verified.

rules_applied.description: Group structure - consignor name found explicitly in invoice
Country verification via indirect identifiers (VAT prefix SE)

consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor 'Esswell International AB' is the issuer of the invoice. The company name 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB' appears explicitly in the invoice footer and body. The address in the invoice footer ('PO Box 11486, Södra Hamngatan 19-21, SE-404 30 Gothenburg, Sweden') matches the certificate address ('Box 11486, Södra Hamngatan 19-21, SE-404 30, GÖTEBORG'). Minor variation: 'GÖTEBORG' vs 'Gothenburg' is the same city (Swedish vs English name). Country 'Sweden' confirmed by explicit mention and VAT prefix SE. MATCH per rule 4.1.0.
consignor.debug_explanation: Exact certificate text: 'Esswell International AB, Box 11486, Södra Hamngatan 19-21, SE-404 30, GÖTEBORG, Sweden'. Exact invoice text: 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB' (footer and body), 'PO Box 11486, Södra Hamngatan 19-21, SE-404 30 Gothenburg, Sweden' (footer). Normalizations: case normalization. Rule 4.1.0 applied as consignor name found explicitly in invoice. Country confirmed via explicit 'Sweden' in address and SE prefix in VAT. Result: MATCH.

Certificate: 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED'. Invoice: 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED'. Exact match after case normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are present in certificate
comparison_steps.details: No article numbers are specified in the certificate. Moving to Priority 2.

comparison_steps.description: Identify identity-bearing main designation and match
comparison_steps.details: Certificate identity-bearing main designation: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER'. Invoice description: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER AS PER PROFORMA INVOICE NO. 508915 DATED 23.01.2023.' The identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' is found exactly at the beginning of the invoice description. The additional text 'AS PER PROFORMA INVOICE NO. 508915 DATED 23.01.2023.' is a non-identity-bearing addition (reference to proforma invoice) per rule 4.3.4.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching - identity-bearing main designation identified and matched exactly
Non-identity-bearing additions - additional proforma invoice reference does not affect product identity

goods_description.motivation: Certificate goods description 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' is found exactly in the invoice description 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER AS PER PROFORMA INVOICE NO. 508915 DATED 23.01.2023.' The identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' matches exactly. The trailing text is a proforma invoice reference which is a non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4. MATCH.
goods_description.debug_explanation: Exact certificate text: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER'. Exact invoice text: 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER AS PER PROFORMA INVOICE NO. 508915 DATED 23.01.2023.' Normalization: case normalization only. Identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' found as exact prefix of invoice description. Rule 4.3.3 and 4.3.4 applied. Result: MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Determine which quantity in box 7 is the verification basis
comparison_steps.details: Box 7 contains: '95,468 MT net/gross' and '77 Rolls'. Both are in box 7. The weight value '95,468 MT net/gross' is the primary quantity. '77 Rolls' is secondary.

Certificate: '95,468 MT net/gross'. Invoice: '95,468 MT net/gross weight'. The numeric value 95,468 (with comma as thousands separator context: in this case, looking at context, 95,468 is 95.468 MT — i.e. comma is decimal separator representing 95.468 metric tons). Invoice shows '95,468 MT net/gross weight'. Same notation, same value. Both indicate net/gross weight category. MATCH.
Certificate: '77 Rolls'. Invoice: '77 Rolls'. Exact match.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check - numeric values identified in invoice
Quantity requirements - both numeric value and unit present in certificate
Verification against invoice - numeric value and unit match

quantity.motivation: Certificate box 7 states '95,468 MT net/gross' and '77 Rolls'. Invoice states '95,468 MT net/gross weight' and '77 Rolls'. The weight value 95,468 MT (using comma as decimal separator, representing 95.468 metric tons) matches exactly between certificate and invoice. The weight category 'net/gross' is stated in both documents. The roll count '77 Rolls' also matches exactly. Both the primary weight quantity and the secondary count are verified. MATCH.
quantity.debug_explanation: Exact certificate text: '95,468 MT net/gross' and '77 Rolls'. Exact invoice text: '95,468 MT net/gross weight' and '77 Rolls'. The comma in '95,468' functions as a decimal separator (95.468 metric tons is a reasonable weight for 77 rolls of testliner). Both documents use the same notation. No conversion or summation needed. Rule 4.4.1 direct occurrence check. Result: MATCH.

comparison_steps.details: Invoice has explicit 'Country of Origin' field with value 'France'.
Certificate: 'FRANCE'. Invoice: 'France'. After case normalization: 'france' = 'france'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Check if EU normalization is needed
comparison_steps.details: Certificate states 'FRANCE' (specific country). Invoice states 'France'. Direct match exists. France is an EU member state (relevant for the EU certificate header), but no EU normalization is needed since both documents name the same specific country. The certificate is issued under 'EUROPEAN UNION' framework but box 3 explicitly states 'FRANCE'. This is verified directly against invoice 'France'.

rules_applied.description: Identification in invoice - explicit Country of Origin field found

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate box 3 states 'FRANCE' as country of origin. Invoice has explicit 'Country of Origin' field with value 'France'. After case normalization, both are 'france'. Direct exact match. MATCH.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Exact certificate text (box 3): 'FRANCE'. Exact invoice text (Country of Origin field): 'France'. Normalization: case only. No EU normalization needed as specific country name matches directly. Result: MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.details: Certificate contains 'Credit No 0026IMU23000002' but 'Credit No' is not one of the explicit banking term labels listed in 2.2. LC exception not activated.
Consignor 'Esswell International AB' found in invoice. Consignee 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED' found in invoice. Order reference '21386' matches. Pairing validated.
Consignor 'Esswell International AB' found explicitly as 'ESSWELL INTERNATIONAL AB' in invoice footer and body. MATCH established at this step; 4.1.3.1-4.1.3.6 not needed.
Sweden confirmed via explicit address mention and VAT prefix SE.
Primary billing field 'Customer/Applicant' contains 'HANSPAUL INDUSTRIES LIMITED'. Used as authoritative consignee.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Controlled text matching for goods description
rule_evaluation_log.details: Identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' found exactly in invoice.
Additional text 'AS PER PROFORMA INVOICE NO. 508915 DATED 23.01.2023.' is a reference addition, not identity-changing.
95,468 MT net/gross and 77 Rolls both found in invoice.
Explicit 'Country of Origin' field in invoice with value 'France'.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: LC exception should be activated based on 'Credit No 0026IMU23000002'
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 2.2, the term 'Credit No' is not one of the explicitly listed banking term labels ('LC', 'L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit', 'LC No.', 'LC Number'). While it likely refers to a documentary credit in context, the strict rule requires one of the enumerated terms. LC exception not activated; full verification performed.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The comma in '95,468' could be a thousands separator (representing 95468 MT)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: In context, 95,468 MT for 77 rolls of testliner paper is a reasonable weight (approximately 1.24 MT per roll). 95,468 MT (ninety-five thousand metric tons) would be an unreasonable weight for 77 rolls. Both certificate and invoice use identical notation '95,468 MT', confirming the comma is a decimal separator representing 95.468 metric tons.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Avsändaren Esswell International AB i Sverige, mottagaren Hanspaul Industries Limited i Tanzania, varubeskrivningen White Top Testliner, kvantiteten 95,468 MT net/gross (77 rullar) samt ursprungslandet Frankrike kan samtliga verifieras i fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints (consignor, consignee, goods description, quantity, country of origin) verified as MATCH. Certificate consignor Esswell International AB (Sweden) confirmed in invoice. Consignee Hanspaul Industries Limited (Tanzania) confirmed in invoice. Goods description 'WHITE TOP TESTLINER' confirmed. Quantity 95,468 MT net/gross and 77 Rolls confirmed. Country of origin France confirmed in explicit origin field. No discrepancies found.
